# UC-15.3.11 · CCTV / IP Camera Health Monitoring

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Availability, Security

*We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.*

---

## Description

Continuous telemetry from data-center CCTV paths that surfaces camera reachability, active recording arms, NVR-attached disk consumption, and last-frame freshness so operators see coverage loss before an investigation needs footage.

## Value

Persistent blind spots weaken physical-access investigations, regulatory evidence requests, and after-hours alarm correlation; correlating NVR-reported camera state with storage headroom protects incident timelines and proves the surveillance control stayed within policy.

## Implementation

Deploy REST polling (Splunkbase #1747 modular input or scripted HEC forwarder) against Hikvision ISAPI, Milestone, or Genetec management APIs plus optional ONVIF metadata pulls; normalize into connection_status, recording_status, storage_util_pct, last_frame_time; schedule polls every 5–15 minutes; alert when cameras are not online, recording is paused, or storage_util_pct exceeds ninety percent; trend uptime by zone and reconcile against rack-level physical-access maps.

## Evidence

Export the alert result set (camera_id, connection_status, recording_status, storage_util_pct, _time) plus a screenshot or API response JSON from Hikvision ISAPI or the VMS UI for the matching window and store it with the physical security case record.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

During a controlled test in a staging cage, disconnect a campus camera Ethernet drop or stop its channel in Milestone Recording Server while leaving the NVR online and TLS polling credentials valid; within two REST modular-input poll cycles spanning dispatch.earliest_time=-15m the collector emits connection_status=offline and recording_status!=recording so the scheduled alert triggers, carries camera_id plus last_frame_time evidence, and matches what Genetec Security Desk shows for that channel.

### Negative scenario

Mark the same camera under a documented change record, pause the REST input for that host group, and feed synthetic keepalives showing connection_status=online with recording_status=recording while the GUI still shows maintenance; the correlation should suppress because the maintenance lookup flag is present, proving the rule is not blindly trust the poller.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Confirm network paths from the Splunk heavy forwarder or input tier to each NVR management VLAN; TLS intercept must not break mutual-auth scenarios used by Milestone or Genetec.
- Install REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase app 1747) or maintain an approved custom collector consistent with Custom (NVR API, ONVIF, Hikvision ISAPI); store credentials via Splunk secrets/RBAC—not props.conf literals.
- Map each manufacturer to its documented HTTP surface (Hikvision ISAPI XML/JSON, Milestone REST, Genetec Web SDK) and translate responses into shared keys connection_status, recording_status, storage_util_pct, last_frame_time, camera_id, location.

Configure data collection
- Create modular-input stanzas that GET management URIs every five to fifteen minutes (tighter during commissioning) and stamp _time from the appliance clock inside the payload.
- Route successful polls to index=physical with sourcetype="nvr:camera_status"; optionally duplicate a summary metric stream for executive dashboards.
- For ONVIF complements, call lightweight device-management actions permitted by your VMS and fold identifiers into the same sourcetype via FIELDALIAS so downstream SPL stays unified.

Search, alerting, and access controls
- Base detection SPL on the existing saved pattern targeting index=physical sourcetype="nvr:camera_status"; schedule production alerts with dispatch.earliest_time=-15m when polls run every five minutes to capture two samples minimum.
- Restrict knowledge objects to a dedicated RBAC role such as physical_security_ops plus delegated admins; reviewers without CCTV clearance should see masked location tokens only.

Validate
- Spot-check five cameras per site against the Milestone Smart Client, Genetec Security Desk, or Hikvision web GUI for the identical poll window; reconcile storage_util_pct with NVR RAID dashboards before trusting thresholds.

Operationalize
- Publish a coverage dashboard (status grid + drill-down table), attach webhook or ticketing alert actions, and document escalation when recording_status stays non-recording beyond vendor SLA timers.

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="nvr:camera_status"
| where connection_status!="online" OR recording_status!="recording" OR storage_util_pct > 90
| table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, storage_util_pct, last_frame_time
| sort connection_status
```

## Visualization

Heatmap matrix of zones × camera health, sortable Table of offline channels with storage_util_pct and last_frame_time, radial Gauge for pooled NVR disk headroom, and optional datacenter floor overlay fed from location metadata.

## Known False Positives

- Milestone Recording Server maintenance toggles archiving policies—suppress alerts when change_ticket=<CHG#####> matches an approved maintenance lookup Join covering that subnet window.
- Genetec failover clusters briefly flip recording_status while VIP migrates—require two consecutive polls offline before paging unless cluster_role=floating_draining.
- Hikvision ISAPI storage_util_pct spikes during RAID rebuild—differentiate rebuild_pct>0 via REST JSON block hddList[].statusStatus before firing critical pages.
- ONVIF-only probes lose connectivity when cameras reboot firmware yet NVR retains healthy upstream streams—cross-reference gateway_keepalive=true from the VMS heartbeat feed to avoid duplicate tickets.
